rationale: NYC commercial real estate requires authority, clarity, and truth. Dark UI feels too 'fintech-bro'. We want the application to feel like a high-end architectural blueprint mixed with a Bloomberg terminal—objective, rigid, and functional.

headings.usage: Use for the massive address search input, report title, and massive BSF metrics.

body.usage: Use for plain english zoning descriptions, lot characteristics, and table data.

mono.usage: CRITICAL: Use for all source citation chips (NYC PLUTO, ACRIS, GeoSearch), and numeric values like Square Footage and Land Value.

rules: Mobile-First: Start with grid-cols-1, move to md:grid-cols-3 or lg:grid-cols-4 for dense data.
Spacing: Generous p-8 or p-12 for the hero search, but tight gap-4 for the bento data cards.
Surfaces: Flat solid backgrounds. NO GLASSMORPHISM. 1px sharp borders. border-radius of max 4px (rounded-sm) for a brutalist, technical feel.
Sidebar: Use a rigid left sidebar for 'Past Lookups' with a subtle border-r.

hero_search.style: Massive typography (text-5xl to text-6xl+). Input field should not look like a standard box; it should be a bottom-bordered clean architectural line or a massive bento block. Auto-complete style.
hero_search.alignment: Left-aligned for authoritative feel. Do not center.

citation_chips.style: Tiny, uppercase, mono font. border border-zinc-200 bg-zinc-100 text-[10px] tracking-widest px-1.5 py-0.5 rounded-sm. Must be placed next to every numeric claim.

data_cards.style: Flat, 1px border. No shadows. Use 'Grid Borders' style: border-r border-b border-border/40 to create a technical table-like feel for the bento boxes.

comparables_table.style: Dense tabular layout. Use standard Shadcn Table but remove rounded corners. Headers in small uppercase mono.

flags_section.style: Use Signal Red (#FF3B30) sparingly for borders or text of the 'things to investigate' section to immediately draw the broker's eye.

interactions.hover: Sharp 1px solid stroke or background inversion. No soft shadows. For rows in tables: hover:bg-zinc-100.
interactions.loading_state: Step-by-step pipeline checklist with mono-spaced ASCII/Terminal feel (Geocoding -> PLUTO -> ACRIS -> Claude). Do not use a generic spinner. Use a progress bar or text terminal output.

image_urls.description: High-contrast black and white tall NYC buildings looking up. Represents scale, development, and zoning.
Modern building facade with unique geometric design.

instructions_to_main_agent: Install required fonts from Google Fonts: 'Cabinet Grotesk' (via Fontshare or local if not available, else substitute with Space Grotesk/Clash Display), 'IBM Plex Sans', and 'IBM Plex Mono'.
Setup a two-pane layout: A narrow left sidebar (w-64) for 'Report History / Past Lookups' and a main content area.

Implement the initial state as a massive, bold search input ('NYC Development Site Analyzer') taking up the main view. When a search is executed, transition to the Loading Pipeline view, then to the Data Dashboard.

The Data Dashboard must use a tightly packed grid (bento 'Control Room' style). Sections: Zoning Summary, Lot Characteristics, Development Potential (Make BSF the largest number on the screen), Comparable Sales, Land Value.
EVERY numeric datapoint must have a citation chip next to it. E.g., '10,000 [NYC PLUTO]'.
Ensure the use of Lucide React icons for the pipeline loading state and sidebar history.
Add 'data-testid' to ALL interactive elements (search input, submit button, history links, citation links).
